material_details.material_description: Silver is a soft, white, lustrous transition metal known for having the highest electrical conductivity, thermal conductivity, and reflectivity of any metal. It is highly ductile and malleable, relatively unreactive, but tarnishes when exposed to air containing sulfur compounds. Its unique properties make it valuable across a wide range of industries.
